rationale: Proof is unprovable as posed. The function's postcondition only constrains abstractify_end_points(vec), which is a Seq<AbstractEndPoint> via v@.map(...). det_parse_end_points_equal requires r1->Some_0 == r2->Some_0 (Vec<EndPoint> equality). In Verus stdlib, Vec equality is NOT view-determined: empirically tested that 'requires a@ == b@ ensures a == b' fails on Vec<u8>. No axiom in vstd::std_specs::vec relates view equality to SMT equality. Two Vec<EndPoint> with identical views can be SMT-distinct, so abstractify equality does not entail Vec equality. Made progress on length and elementwise abstract view equality but cannot bridge the Vec equality gap without forbidden axioms (assume/external_body).
